questions.question: What is the primary function of the Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU) in a Central Processing Unit (CPU)?
options: To store program instructions and data.
To manage data input and output operations.
To perform arithmetic calculations (e.g., addition, subtraction) and logical operations (e.g., AND, OR).
To control the sequence of operations and instruction execution.
To provide temporary storage for frequently accessed data.

questions.question: During the Design for Testability (DFT) phase of an integrated circuit, what is the main purpose of "Boundary Scan" (specified by the IEEE 1149.1 JTAG standard)?
options: To reduce the overall power consumption of the chip during operation.
To enable external access to I/O pins and internal chip functionality for testing and debugging without requiring direct physical probing.
To optimize the physical placement of logic gates for improved performance.
To verify the functional correctness of the Register-Transfer Level (RTL) design against its specifications.
To synthesize the clock network for proper timing distribution.

questions.question: Which type of hazard in a pipelined processor occurs when an instruction attempts to use a hardware resource that is currently occupied by another instruction in the pipeline (e.g., a specific functional unit or memory port)?
options: Data Hazard
Control Hazard
Structural Hazard
Memory Hazard
Register Hazard

questions.question: During the fabrication process of an integrated circuit, what phenomenon primarily leads to unavoidable, statistical variations in transistor threshold voltage (Vt) and current (Id) due to the discrete nature of dopant atoms, becoming a significant challenge for advanced technology nodes?
options: Electromigration
Hot Carrier Injection (HCI)
Negative Bias Temperature Instability (NBTI)
Random Dopant Fluctuations (RDF)
Dielectric Breakdown

questions.question: What is the primary role of a "Program Counter" (PC) in a typical CPU architecture?
options: To store the results of arithmetic and logic operations after execution.
To hold the memory address of the next instruction to be fetched and executed.
To manage the flow of data between the CPU's registers and the main memory.
To contain microcode instructions that define complex CPU operations.
To provide temporary, high-speed storage for operand values during computation.
